title: Mullerian Mimicry Explained
script_summary: A concise explanation of Mullerian mimicry highlighting its role in nature.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you some creatures wear the same dangerous costume?
This is called Mullerian mimicry—where harmful species share similar warning signals.
This strategy benefits them all. Predators learn to avoid their shared colors!
Examples? Think of butterflies, wasps, and even some frogs—all sharing bright warning patterns.
But here's the twist—this mimicry isn't always perfect. Uncertainty lurks in nature!
Curious for more science secrets? Hit that like button and follow for the next mystery!
